title: Knowledge Publish Workflow

story.i_want_to: review and publish draft knowledge articles generated by the self-learning pipeline
story.so_that: I maintain quality control over the knowledge wiki while benefiting from automated article generation

rationale: Automated knowledge extraction produces draft articles that vary in quality. A publish workflow gives tenant administrators the ability to review, curate, and control what enters the published knowledge base. This human-in-the-loop step prevents low-quality or incorrect articles from polluting the wiki while keeping the automation benefits. Bulk publish support enables efficient batch curation when many drafts accumulate, and the drafts listing provides a dedicated review queue with source traceability.

acceptance_criteria.description: POST /api/v1/articles/:id/publish endpoint exists (role: user+). Transitions article status from :draft to :published. Returns 200 with updated article. Returns 422 if article is not in :draft status.

POST /api/v1/articles/:id/unpublish endpoint exists (role: user+). Transitions article status from :published back to :draft. Returns 200 with updated article. Returns 422 if article is not in :published status.

POST /api/v1/knowledge/bulk-publish endpoint exists (role: user+). Accepts JSON body `{"article_ids": ["id1", "id2", ...]}`. Maximum 100 article_ids per request. Returns 422 if the array exceeds 100 elements. This prevents database connection pool exhaustion from a single massive transaction. Publishes all specified articles atomically (Ecto.Multi -- all succeed or all fail). Returns 200 with `{"published_count": N, "articles": [...]}`. Returns 422 if any article is not in :draft status, with error identifying which article(s) failed.

GET /api/v1/knowledge/drafts endpoint exists (role: user+). Returns draft articles ordered by inserted_at desc. Each article includes source_type and source_id for traceability. Supports pagination via limit/offset query params (defaults: limit 20, offset 0).

Only published articles appear in the existing search and index endpoints (GET /api/v1/articles, GET /api/v1/knowledge/search). Draft, archived, and superseded articles are excluded from these endpoints.

Valid status transitions are enforced: draft <-> published (bidirectional), published -> archived (one-way), any status -> superseded (only via ArticleLink creation, not direct API call). Returns 422 with descriptive error for invalid transitions.
An audit event is created for each publish/unpublish action: event_type "knowledge.article_published" or "knowledge.article_unpublished", payload includes article_id and previous status.

POST /api/v1/articles/:id/archive endpoint exists (role: user+). Transitions article to :archived. Returns 422 if the article is in :superseded status. Articles in :draft or :published status can be archived.
All endpoints enforce tenant scoping via RLS. A tenant can only publish/unpublish/archive their own articles.
Bulk publish validates that all article_ids belong to the requesting tenant. Returns 404 if any article_id is not found (RLS will naturally enforce this).
Routes are added to the router under the existing /api/v1 scope with appropriate auth pipeline.

A Loopctl.Knowledge.Article.valid_transition?/2 function exists that validates status transitions. Valid transitions: draft->published, published->draft, published->archived, draft->archived, superseded->draft. Any status can transition to :superseded (via ArticleLink only, not direct API). superseded->draft is valid (enables recovery when a superseding article is later found incorrect). When a supersedes link is deleted, the administrator can manually restore the target article to :draft status for re-review. The function is tested independently with unit tests.

test_cases.name: Reject publish for non-draft article
expected_results: Response 422 with error indicating invalid status transition

test_cases.name: Bulk publish multiple draft articles atomically
expected_results: All three articles have status: :published in database

test_cases.name: Bulk publish fails atomically when one article is not draft
preconditions: Two draft articles and one published article
steps: POST /api/v1/knowledge/bulk-publish with all three article IDs
expected_results: Response 422 with error identifying the non-draft article

test_cases.name: Drafts listing returns only draft articles with source traceability
preconditions: Two draft articles and one published article for tenant
expected_results: Each article includes source_type and source_id fields

test_cases.name: Tenant isolation: cannot publish another tenant's article
expected_results: Response 404 -- article not found via RLS

test_cases.name: Unpublish a published article returns to draft

technical_notes: Status transition logic should be encapsulated in a private function in the Knowledge context module, e.g., `valid_transition?(:draft, :published) -> true`. This keeps transition rules centralized and testable.
Bulk publish MUST use Ecto.Multi to ensure atomicity. Pattern: iterate article_ids, add a Multi step for each update, execute the Multi. If any step fails, all roll back.
The drafts endpoint is separate from GET /api/v1/articles because the main articles endpoint should filter to published-only. Drafts is a dedicated review queue.
Controller actions should live in a KnowledgeController or ArticleWorkflowController, following the existing LoopctlWeb controller naming pattern.
Audit events follow the existing AuditLog pattern: created in the same Multi as the status change for atomicity.
Testing: async: true, ConnCase for controller tests, fixture(:article) with status params, tenant isolation test, setup :verify_on_exit!.
